weather_report:
Late morning in Wembley arrives with a shy sun pressing through scattered clouds, the light clean and gentle, edges on buildings drawn in crisp relief. The day looks clear and settled, the sort where shadows come and go as the sky rearranges itself. UV will build toward the middle of the day, not fierce but persuasive enough to ask for sunglasses and a quick brush of sunscreen if you linger. It feels like a day made for ease.

Expect the afternoon to keep this rhythm of bright spells and passing cloud, pleasant for moving about without breaking a sweat. Dress in light layers—breathable shirt, a cardigan or thin jacket you can forget on the back of a chair—and comfortable shoes for unhurried errands. A short walk to pick up fruit, a quiet lap around the park, or tending to balcony plants would fit the mood; later, a window-side lunch or a book on a bench while the sun winks in and out. The sort of weather that doesn’t demand attention, only rewards it.
